user_query:
We just discovered our OpenClaw gateway config might be unsafe. Please read input/openclaw.json and produce a hardened plan and artifacts. Deliver all files exactly at the following paths under output/:

1) output/openclaw.secure.json — Start from the input config and apply secure defaults. Requirements:
- gateway.bind must be set to "loopback".
- gateway.port must remain 18789.
- gateway.auth.mode must be "token" and gateway.auth.token must be a freshly generated 64-character hex string (no placeholders or comments).
- Add a Tailscale section with tailscale.mode set to "serve".
- Do not remove any unrelated top-level settings you see in the input; only harden security-relevant fields.

2) output/permissions_fix.txt — A short list of exact shell commands to lock down sensitive files and directories. Include these lines verbatim (each on its own line):
- chmod 700 ~/.openclaw
- chmod 600 ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json
- chmod 700 ~/.openclaw/credentials

3) output/ufw_rules.md — A deny-by-default firewall policy as explicit command lines, allowing only outbound traffic, SSH, and (if used) Tailscale. Include these exact lines somewhere:
- sudo ufw default deny incoming
- sudo ufw default allow outgoing
- sudo ufw allow ssh
- sudo ufw enable
If you include Tailscale, also include: sudo ufw allow in on tailscale0. Do not include any command that opens the gateway port (e.g., do not include "sudo ufw allow 18789"). Add 1–3 sentences explaining why the gateway port should not be opened.

4) output/mdns_disable.txt — A single export line to disable mDNS/Bonjour broadcasting: export CLAWDBOT_DISABLE_BONJOUR=1

5) output/audit_plan.md — A step-by-step hardening audit plan tailored to the provided config. Must include: (a) verifying the runtime is on Node.js v22.12.0+ (do not list install commands), (b) running a deep security audit, (c) SSH hardening guidance with a clear warning about potential lockouts and how to avoid them, and (d) rollback/verification notes.

Please keep everything in plain English. Do not alter input files. All outputs must be text or JSON as specified and placed under output/.
